qa_pairs.question: What steps should I take before contacting AllianceCan support, and what makes a support request more effective for faster assistance?
qa_pairs.answer: Before writing to AllianceCan support, consider checking the system status page to see if your problem has already been reported, and ensure that the email address from which you are writing is registered in your account so the ticketing system can recognize you automatically. For faster and more accurate assistance, write a well-described question or problem, include the system/cluster name and a few words about what may be wrong in the email subject line (e.g., "Job 123456 fails to run on the Rorqual cluster"), and always start a brand new email thread for a different topic instead of replying to an old one.

qa_pairs.question: What specific information should be included in a support request to AllianceCan to ensure staff can provide the best possible help?
qa_pairs.answer: To help AllianceCan staff assist you better, your support request should include the cluster name, job ID, your job submission script (either the full path, copied, or attached), the file or files containing error message(s) (full path, copied, or attached), the commands you were executing, the software name and version you were trying to use, and when the problem happened. Additionally, if you want staff to access, copy, edit your files, or inspect and possibly make changes to your account, you must state this explicitly in your email. Remember never to send your password, and the maximum attachment size is 40 MB.

qa_pairs.question: Which email address should I use when contacting AllianceCan technical support for different types of issues?
qa_pairs.answer: You should choose the email address that best corresponds to your specific question or issue: use `accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca` for account questions, `renewals@tech.alliancecan.ca` for account renewals, `globus@tech.alliancecan.ca` for Globus file transfer services, `cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca` for Cloud resources, `allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca` for questions about the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC), `trillium@tech.alliancecan.ca` specifically for the Trillium cluster, and `support@tech.alliancecan.ca` for any other general question or issue.
